senses.definition: a field painted with parallel lines in which American football is played

examples.x: The players lined up on the gridiron, ready for the kickoff to start the football game.
The gridiron was carefully maintained, with the lines freshly painted before each game to ensure clarity and visibility for the players and officials.
He surveyed the gridiron from the sidelines, strategizing with his coaching staff on the best plays to outmaneuver the opposing team.
The stadium erupted with cheers as the home team scored a touchdown, crossing the goal line at the end of the gridiron.
The annual Thanksgiving Day game was a tradition cherished by fans, who gathered to watch their favorite teams battle it out on the gridiron.

senses.definition: a platform surrounded with ropes on which boxing and wrestling competitions take place
a designated area or facility where shooting sports such as archery, shooting, or hunting are practiced

examples.x: They visited the archery range to hone their skills before the tournament.
The military conducts training and shooting exercises at the outdoor range.
The Olympic shooting team trained rigorously at the national range.
The club members gathered at the indoor range for a shooting competition.

senses.definition: an area where people can play basketball, tennis, etc.

examples.x: They spent the afternoon playing volleyball on the sandy court.
The school's new court is perfect for both basketball and tennis.

senses.definition: the infield area of a baseball or softball field, typically shaped like a square with four bases at its corners

examples.x: The pitcher threw a strike right across the diamond.
The shortstop made a diving catch in the infield diamond.
He hit a home run that sailed over the outfield fence and landed outside the diamond.
The runner slid into third base, kicking up dust on the diamond.
The coach signaled for a steal of home plate, sending the runner across the diamond.

senses.definition: a gentle and easy ski slope, typically used by beginners

examples.x: The bunny slope offers a safe environment for beginners to practice.
We can meet at the bottom of the bunny slope after our ski lesson.
She took a few spills but eventually mastered skiing on the bunny slope.
Ski instructors often teach new techniques on the bunny slope.
We spent the morning on the bunny slope before moving to the intermediate slopes.

senses.definition: the area on a mountain or hill where people are allowed to ski
a designated bench or area where baseball players and coaches sit during a game when they are not actively playing, typically located near the diamond

examples.x: Players in the dugout cheered after a home run.
He quietly waited in the dugout for his next turn at bat.
The coach gathered the team in the dugout during a timeout.
She grabbed her glove and hurried out of the dugout to play left field.
The players kept their gear organized in the dugout between innings.

senses.definition: a designated area beyond the outfield where relief pitchers warm up before entering a game

examples.x: The pitchers in bullpen cheered as their teammate struck out the side in the inning.
The pitcher warmed up in the bullpen before being called into the game.
The closer in the bullpen prepared to enter the game in the ninth inning.
The coach signaled for a reliever to start warming up in the bullpen.
The manager visited the bullpen to discuss strategy with the relief pitchers.

senses.definition: an area of land or water used for races, sports, and other similar activities

examples.x: The ski course wound its way through the mountain, offering breathtaking views to the adventurous skiers.
The equestrian course featured a series of jumps and obstacles, testing both horse and rider's agility and precision.
The soccer course was lined with vibrant green turf, where players honed their dribbling and passing skills.

senses.definition: an area located next to the playing area in sports like basketball or tennis, offering a close view of the game

examples.x: The coach stood near the courtside, shouting instructions to the players.
She enjoyed watching the game from the courtside, as it gave her a perfect view.

senses.definition: a marked and groomed trail or slope prepared for skiing and snowboarding

examples.x: Safety signs along the piste reminded skiers to maintain control.
Skiers enjoyed the wide, open piste under the clear blue sky.
The beginners stayed on the gentle piste to practice their turns.
Fresh snow made the piste perfect for carving turns.
The snowboarders raced down the challenging piste during the competition.

senses.definition: a marked trail or slope for skiing, ranging in difficulty from beginner to advanced
the course between the tee and the green that is well-maintained for easier play

examples.x: Golfers aim to hit the fairway off the tee for a better chance at a par or birdie.
His drive landed perfectly in the middle of the fairway.
The fairway stretches for several hundred yards before reaching the green.
The golfers walked down the fairway toward their balls.
He hit his approach shot from the fairway onto the green.

senses.definition: a type of golf course located near the sea

examples.x: He enjoys playing on the links because of the ocean breeze.
They walked along the links, enjoying the scenic views.

senses.definition: a small area near the hole where golfers practice their short, precise shots

examples.x: The tournament featured tricky pin placements on the putting green.
Her ball stopped inches from the hole on the putting green.
They redesigned the putting green for more challenging play.
Golfers gathered around the putting green to discuss strategy.
He spent hours on the putting green, refining his technique.

senses.definition: a tennis court surfaced with crushed brick or clay

examples.x: She slipped on the clay court during the match.
The French Open is one of the most prestigious tennis tournaments played on clay courts.
Clay courts require different playing strategies than hard courts.
The ball leaves distinct marks on the clay court.
Rafael Nadal's dominance in tennis is especially evident on clay courts.

senses.definition: the area directly next to the boxing or wrestling ring where spectators, officials, coaches, and support staff sit during the match

examples.x: The announcer introduced the fighters as they entered the ringside.
Fans cheered loudly in the ringside as the wrestling match reached its climax.
The celebrity was seen enjoying the fight from the ringside.
The trainer was shouting instructions to his fighter from ringside.
The photographer captured some incredible shots from ringside during the match.

senses.definition: a seat close to the ring where spectators have a clear view of the wrestling or boxing match

examples.x: He had a ringside seat for the championship boxing match.
The VIPs enjoyed ringside seats, just feet away from the action.
She cheered loudly from her ringside seat as her favorite wrestler entered the ring.
Fans with ringside seats could feel the intensity of every punch.
The commentator provided live coverage from his ringside seat.

senses.definition: a tennis court made of concrete or asphalt, offering a fast playing surface

examples.x: The hard court's consistent bounce allows for precise tennis shots.
After the rain, they had to dry the hard court before resuming their tennis match.
His favorite tennis shoes provided excellent traction on the hard court.
She practiced her serves and volleys on the hard court every afternoon.
The hard court surface can be tough on the knees during long tennis matches.

senses.definition: small enclosed area or field where horses are kept, exercised, or prepared before a race

examples.x: The trainers inspected the horses in the paddock before the race.
She walked her horse around the paddock to warm up before the competition.
They fenced off the paddock to separate the horses during feeding time.
The veterinarian checked each horse's health in the paddock.
Race officials lined up the horses in the paddock according to their post positions.

senses.definition: areas outside the playing boundaries where the game cannot be played
the part of the baseball outfield directly between left field and right field, where a center fielder plays

examples.x: He sprinted to center field to track down the deep fly ball.
Fans cheered as the outfielder leaped against the center field wall to make the catch.
The baseball was hit deep into center field, nearly reaching the fence.
The center field area is expansive, requiring quick players to cover ground.
He aimed for the gap in center field, hoping to score a double.

senses.definition: the area of the baseball outfield positioned to the right of the second baseman when facing the outfield from home plate

examples.x: He hit a towering fly ball into right field.
The ball rolled into the corner of right field, allowing the batter to reach second base.
The sun can be challenging for outfielders in right field during late afternoon games.
The grounds crew watered the grass in right field to keep it green and lush.
The team celebrated their win with a group photo in right field after the game.

senses.definition: the area of the baseball outfield positioned to the left of the second baseman when facing the outfield from home plate

examples.x: He hit a deep fly ball into left field.
The left field area is shaded by the scoreboard during evening games.
A foul ball landed in the stands behind left field, narrowly missing a fan.
The grounds crew trimmed the grass in left field to maintain a uniform playing surface.
The ball bounced off the wall in left field, allowing the batter to reach second base.

senses.definition: one of the four designated areas in the ring where a fighter's team provides support and guidance between rounds

examples.x: The boxer returned to his corner to rest between rounds.
Her coach gave her advice from the corner.
Between rounds, he sipped water in his corner.
She sat in her corner, listening to her trainer.
The medical team present in his corner quickly tended to his cuts and bruises.

senses.definition: the area where the outfielders play to catch fly balls and field hits
the area within the diamond-shaped boundaries formed by the four bases, where the infielders play

examples.x: The ground ball was fielded cleanly by the shortstop in the infield.
The pitcher delivered a strike right down the middle of the infield.
He slid into second base, kicking up dirt on the infield.
The third baseman made a diving stop in the infield to save a run.

senses.definition: a marked area near the goal, especially in hockey or lacrosse
the area of the court nearest to a team's own basket or goal, primarily used in basketball and tennis

examples.x: The point guard dribbled the ball in the backcourt.
They set up a defensive trap in the backcourt.
She sprinted back to defend in the backcourt.
He passed the ball to his teammate in the backcourt.
He committed a backcourt violation by stepping over the line.

senses.definition: the area near the front of the court, typically used in tennis and badminton

examples.x: The forecourt is a crucial area for controlling the pace of the game.
She positioned herself in the forecourt to volley the ball.
He attacked his opponent's weak return from the forecourt.
The shuttlecock landed just inside the forecourt.
The player's aggressive play in the forecourt put pressure on his opponent.

senses.definition: the specific area on the court from which a player must serve in sports like badminton and tennis

examples.x: The player served from the back service court.
They focused on accuracy when serving into the service court.
The player's powerful serve reached the back of the service court.
She aimed for the corner of the service court with her serve.
He faulted by serving into the wrong service court.

senses.definition: an artificial surface designed to simulate the experience of skiing without the need for snow
an area where spectators sit or stand to watch the game

examples.x: The fans filled the stands to cheer for their team.
She waved enthusiastically from the stands as her favorite player scored a goal.
The stadium's upper stands offered a great view of the entire field.
Spectators packed the stands for the championship game.
The announcer's voice echoed through the stands, announcing the starting lineup.

senses.definition: the area at each end of the field where players score touchdowns

examples.x: The wide receiver sprinted into the end zone for a touchdown.
They celebrated wildly after reaching the end zone.
The team's strategy focused on getting the ball into the end zone quickly.
The offense lined up just outside the opponent's end zone.

senses.definition: (ice hockey) the area between the two blue lines on the rink where neither team has possession advantage

examples.x: The defenseman intercepted the puck in the neutral zone.
He dumped the puck into the neutral zone to relieve pressure.
They clogged up the neutral zone to disrupt the opponent's offense.
The centerman won the faceoff in the neutral zone.
They turned over the puck in the neutral zone, leading to a scoring chance.

senses.definition: (American football) the area between the opponent's 20-yard line and the goal line, where scoring becomes a priority

examples.x: The team struggled to convert in the red zone.
The red zone is where games are often won or lost.
Their offense struggled to move the ball into the red zone.
A turnover in the red zone can swing the momentum of the game.
He analyzed the opponent's defense to exploit weaknesses in the red zone.

senses.definition: the designated mark from which penalty kicks are taken

examples.x: The referee pointed to the penalty spot after the foul.
He nervously approached the penalty spot, eyeing the goalkeeper.
The penalty spot, marked clearly on the field, awaited the crucial kick.
From the penalty spot, she expertly placed the ball into the top corner.
He confidently placed the ball on the penalty spot.

senses.definition: an area shaped like a rectangle that is made for playing tennis

examples.x: The local community center recently renovated its tennis court, adding new surfaces and lighting to accommodate evening matches.
She practiced her serves and volleys on the tennis court every afternoon, determined to improve her game before the upcoming tournament.
The tennis court was bustling with activity as players of all ages participated in lessons and friendly matches throughout the day.
After a long day at work, he found relaxation on the tennis court, enjoying a friendly game with his neighbor.
The championship match was held on the center tennis court, where spectators gathered to watch the top players compete for the title.

senses.definition: a rectangular playing surface with baskets or hoops at each end, used for playing the sport of basketball

examples.x: The basketball court was packed with players during the tournament.
He scored the winning shot from the three-point line on the basketball court.
The coach emphasized teamwork and passing on the basketball court.
The basketball court has a wooden floor for better traction.
The local gym has a basketball court available for public use.

senses.definition: a designated rectangular area with specific markings where the game of badminton is played

examples.x: They set up the net on the badminton court for their match.
She practiced her serves on the badminton court every evening.
The badminton court was newly painted with clear boundary lines.
He booked the badminton court for an hour to play with his friends.
The shuttlecock landed just inside the line on the badminton court.

senses.definition: the central area of an ice hockey rink, typically marked by a red line

examples.x: The goalie made a spectacular save from center ice.
The referee dropped the puck at center ice to start the period.
The players lined up at center ice for the opening face-off.
He fired a slap shot from center ice, surprising the goalie.
Fans cheered as the player skated through center ice on a breakaway.

senses.definition: the designated area on the ground where skydivers land after their parachute descent
the area surrounding a swimming pool where spectators or swimmers relax or watch activities

examples.x: Lifeguards monitored the poolside to ensure safety.
The poolside bar served refreshing drinks to swimmers and sunbathers.
Families enjoyed lounging poolside on a sunny afternoon.
She applied sunscreen before sitting poolside to avoid sunburn.

senses.definition: the rectangular area in front of the goal where goal kicks are taken and certain rules apply to player positioning during corner kicks

examples.x: The goalkeeper positioned herself to defend the goal area during the corner kick.
Players crowded the goal area, hoping to score from the corner kick.
She directed her header toward the goal area, aiming for a teammate.
The referee blew the whistle for a foul committed inside the goal area.
He cleared the ball out of the goal area to prevent a scoring opportunity.

senses.definition: the center point of the American football field, marking the midway point between each team's end zone
the area of the volleyball court where a team's setter is not positioned

examples.x: When the setter is on the weak side, the hitters need to adjust their positions accordingly.
The weak side can become a target for aggressive serves.
If the ball goes to the weak side, the setter must quickly decide on the best play.
Hitters should be prepared to attack from the weak side when the opportunity arises.
Coaches often emphasize the importance of defense on the weak side.

senses.definition: (baseball) any of the four stations that the runner must reach to score a run
one of the four bases on the diamond-shaped field that runners must touch in sequence to score a run

examples.x: The infielders shifted their positioning depending on whether there was a runner on second base.
The player reached second base standing up after a well-executed hit.
The batter hit a ground ball that deflected off the pitcher's glove and rolled to second base.
The player took a lead off second base, anticipating a steal attempt.
The outfielder's throw sailed over second base, allowing the runner to advance to third.

senses.definition: the base that a baserunner must touch third in order to score a run

examples.x: He slid into third base just ahead of the throw.
The ball bounced off the bag at third base and into left field.
The coach signaled for the runner to hold up at third base.
The batter hit a triple, driving in two runs from third base.
The ground crew chalked the lines around third base before the game.

senses.definition: a track or course specifically designed and used for racing events, such as automobile races or horse races

examples.x: The raceway featured multiple turns and straightaways for competitive racing.
Drivers adjusted their strategies based on the raceway's unique layout.
The raceway circuit included challenging elevation changes.
Spectators gathered at the raceway to witness high-speed racing action.
The raceway hosted a series of qualifying heats to determine starting positions.

senses.definition: the area along the track where teams quickly service cars with refueling, tire changes, and adjustments during a race

examples.x: The car sped into the pit for a tire change.
Mechanics rushed to fix an issue in the pit.
Safety gear was ready in the pit.
Pit was bustling with activity as cars entered and exited for service.

senses.definition: the area just outside the playing field where non-players, substitutes, and spectators stay during a sports game

examples.x: The coach paced nervously along the sideline, shouting instructions to his team.
Injured players watched the game from the sideline, eager to get back on the field.
Fans crowded the sideline, cheering loudly for their favorite team.
The substitutes warmed up near the sideline, ready to enter the game at any moment.
A water cooler was set up on the sideline for players to stay hydrated during breaks.

senses.definition: (cricket) the half of the field behind the batsman's legs, toward the left from the batsman's viewpoint for a right-handed batsman
the part of a bowling lane where a player walks before releasing the ball

examples.x: She stepped onto the approach confidently.
The bowler slipped on the approach, causing a foul.
She focused on her footwork on the approach.
The oil pattern on the approach affected his ball's reaction.
The approach was freshly oiled before the league competition.

senses.definition: the ring or arena used in sumo wrestling matches
(golf) the specially prepared and well-maintained area surrounding the hole

examples.x: They redesigned the green to increase the challenge for golfers.
He made a precise putt on the green to save par.
The golf ball rolled smoothly across the green and into the cup.
The tournament organizers placed the pin in a difficult position on the green.
Players should avoid walking on the green to maintain its condition.

senses.definition: the marked area where players start each hole, with different levels of difficulty

examples.x: Golfers must check in at the clubhouse before heading to the tee
He aimed his shot down the middle of the fairway from the tee.
The tee area was surrounded by trees and bunkers.
The golfer positioned himself at the tee, preparing for his first drive.
Golfers gather their equipment at the tee before starting.

senses.definition: a track designed for racing, featuring a series of turns and straight sections that drivers navigate in a loop

examples.x: He raced his car around the famous Silverstone circuit.
The race was held on a challenging circuit in Monaco.
The circuit in Bahrain is known for its high-speed corners.
Spectators enjoyed the race from the stands overlooking the circuit.
The Indianapolis Motor Speedway is a historic oval circuit where the Indy 500 takes place annually.

senses.definition: a course that is used for racing, usually round, and with multiple lanes

examples.x: The athletes warmed up on the track before the race.
He ran five laps around the track during practice.
The track is made of synthetic material for better grip.
She competed in the 400-meter sprint on the track.
The school installed a new track for their athletics program.

senses.definition: a special surface for athletes to run on, which is made of a rubbery material

examples.x: The athletes lined up at the starting line on the running track, ready for the race to begin.
The coach told the runners to focus on their form as they sprinted around the running track.
She trains every morning on the running track to prepare for the upcoming competition.
He enjoyed the peaceful atmosphere of the running track early in the morning, with hardly anyone around.
The school upgraded the running track to make it safer and more comfortable for students.

senses.definition: the area where the fencing bout takes place, marked with boundary lines for the competitors

examples.x: The fencers stepped onto the piste, ready for their match.
She moved swiftly up and down the piste, trying to outmaneuver her opponent.
The referee signaled the start of the bout by calling the fencers to the center of the piste.
He defended his position on the piste with precise footwork and blade control.
The fencer lunged forward, aiming to score a touch on his opponent's chest on the piste.

senses.definition: a type of racing road designed for high-speed car, motorcycle, or even bicycle events, typically oval-shaped with banked turns

examples.x: The speedway hosted an intense race with cars reaching speeds over 200 miles per hour.
They modified the car's setup for better performance on the speedway.
Speedways vary in length and banking angles, influencing racing strategy.
The motorcycle speedway race drew a large crowd of spectators.
She experienced the adrenaline rush of racing on the speedway for the first time.
